summary: Executed backend contract tests against public URL for auth register/me and sex-based personalization; performed static Flutter auth/deep-link/personalization wiring review.

integration_issues.issue: Onboarding screen and plans repository operate on local Drift data and do not call backend /api/onboarding/recommendations or /api/onboarding/complete, so mobile app can diverge from backend personalization contract.

action_items: Wire Flutter onboarding plan recommendation/complete flow to backend endpoints for source-of-truth personalization parity.
Decide authoritative source (backend vs local Drift) for plan personalization and keep one contract path.
Keep deep-link scheme/host aligned across AppConfig, AndroidManifest, and iOS Info.plist when changing OAuth callback.
critical_code_review_comments: Auth API contract matches current mobile client expectations: register/login/google session return {user, session_token}; /auth/me returns {user} and works with Bearer token.
Deep-link callback config appears internally consistent (scheme=habitz, host=auth-callback) across Flutter + Android + iOS.
Primary risk is architectural mismatch: backend personalization exists but Flutter onboarding currently persists local-only personalization and local plan filtering.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 5 backend contract tests passed on public endpoint. No API mock used. Next validation should focus on end-to-end mobile runtime once Flutter SDK/device runner is available, especially onboarding->plans consistency against backend data.
rca of the issue: RCA: personalization mismatch risk is caused by split data sources. Backend computes sex-filtered recommendations/plans, while Flutter onboarding and plans listing currently rely on local Drift repository and local profile state. Repro (static): onboarding_screen.dart uses plansRepository.watchPlans(...) and _persistOnboarding() writes local profile/habits/active plan without backend onboarding API calls. Mitigation: route onboarding preference submission and recommendation retrieval through backend APIs, then sync local cache from backend responses.
